title: A Campus Novel For a Post-Ironic World
description:
Avigayl Sharp’s Offseason is a deeply internal debut, charting the bounds of a violent, unpredictable world through its truth-seeking (and perpetually dishonest) narrator. The novel follows an unnamed woman during her year as an instructor at a remote all girls school, where she’s filling in for an older male teacher on leave for an unspecified […]

The post A Campus Novel For a Post-Ironic World
hook: A woman performs normalcy at a remote girls' school while the world outside grows increasingly unhinged—a debut that treats dishonesty as philosophy.

title: Novelists Tayari Jones and Meg Wolitzer share notes on mothers in fiction
description: Ahead of Mother's Day, we brought together two novelists -- and friends -- Tayari Jones and Meg Wolitzer, to talk about what they love about reading and writing about mothers.
hook: Tayari Jones and Meg Wolitzer trade notes on writing mothers as complex, infuriating, human—not symbols or plot devices.

title: Four Poems
description:
‘allowing someone else / to make small decisions for me / feels slightly erotic’

Four poems by Rae Armantrout.

The post Four Poems appeared first on Granta.
hook: Rae Armantrout writes about the eroticism of relinquishing control, the weight of small decisions, and what bodies want when minds aren't watching.

title: Kokuho review – a kabuki star is born
description: Lee Sang-il's historical drama about the making of a kabuki legend is nothing short of intoxicating.
hook: Lee Sang-il's kabuki drama is intoxicating—a sensory immersion in the making of a legend, all gesture and restraint.

title: Judd Apatow on How He Dug Into the Funniest Man in the World, Mel Brooks
description: The filmmaker joins “Screen Talk” to talk about his two-part HBO documentary and how he grilled the multi-hyphenate to figure out what makes him tick.
hook: Judd Apatow interrogates what actually makes Mel Brooks tick, moving past comedy into the psychology of the funny man.

title: ISpyForGood
description: "On any given day I was seen as both valuable and disposable, sometimes oscillating between these in the same hour."
hook: She was simultaneously valuable and disposable, sometimes switching roles within the same hour—a memoir about the precarity of paid care work.

title: The Making of a Maintenance Artist
description: A new documentary traces Mierle Laderman Ukeles’s decades-long practice of spotlighting marginal, unpaid, and feminine labor.
hook: Mierle Laderman Ukeles spent decades making art about garbage collection and janitorial work—a documentary on the artist who saw invisibility as her subject.
